content.problemStatement:
You are given two binary arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN (N≥3)(N \geq 3)(N≥3).

You can perform the following operation on array AAA:


Select three indexes i,j,i, j,i,j, and kkk (1≤i<j<k≤N)(1 \le i \lt j \lt k \le N)(1≤i<j<k≤N), 

and set Aj=AiA_j=A_iAj​=Ai​ ∣|∣ AjA_jAj​ ∣|∣ AkA_kAk​. Here ∣|∣ denotes the bitwise OR operation.
Determine whether it is possible to convert the array AAA to array BBB by applying any (possibly zero) number of given operations.

Note that, in a binary array each element is either 000 or 111.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — size of arrays.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​, the elements of array AAA.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,B3,…,BNB_1, B_2, B_3,\ldots,B_NB1​,B2​,B3​,…,BN​, the elements of array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output `YES`, it is possible to convert the array AAA to array BBB using any number of given operations. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You can output each character of the answer in uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and YES are considered the same.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 5·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N (N ≥ 3). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN (each 0 or 1). The third line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN (each 0 or 1).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if array A can be transformed into B using any number of allowed operations, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: ends match, interior 0 can be turned to 1 because A already contains a 1, and no 1→0 transition is required → YES.\nTest case 2: first element differs (1 vs 0) → NO.\nTest case 3: A has no 1, but B has a 1 at position 2, which cannot be created → NO.
A is all zeros, B is identical, no changes needed.

solution.approach:
The operation can only change the middle element of a chosen triple to the bitwise OR of the three elements. Since the arrays are binary, the OR becomes 1 if at least one of the three values is 1, otherwise 0. Consequently:
- No element can ever change from 1 to 0.
- Only positions 2 … N‑1 can be modified (position 1 and N are never the middle element).
- If the original array A contains at least one 1, any interior position can be turned into 1 by picking a triple that includes that 1 and any other two indices.
Thus the reachable configurations are characterised by:
1. The first and last elements must already be equal (they never change).
2. No interior position may change from 1 to 0 (i.e., if Ai = 1 then Bi must be 1).
3. If A has no 1 at all, the whole array stays all‑zero, so B must also be all‑zero.
If the above conditions hold, the transformation is possible; otherwise it is impossible.
The algorithm scans the array once per test case, checking the three conditions, which is O(N) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O( Σ N ) ≤ 5·10^5.
